attacking_style: Does not initiate explosive attacks; instead rides calculated breakaway tempo to accumulate time gains. Prioritizes GC positioning over stage hunting when in podium contention.
team_tactics: EF Education uses breakaways strategically for GC positioning rather than stage wins, willing to sacrifice victory chances to maximize overall classification gains through time accumulation.
terrain_preference: Suited to medium-mountain stages where breakaway tempo can yield GC time. Exposed on hard category one finales and steep final climbs where pure climbing ability determines outcomes.
vulnerability: Lacks explosive climbing power on category one finales and steep summit finishes. Gets distanced when pure climbers accelerate, revealing inadequate gradient resistance for sustained GC protection.
key_patterns: Struggles on category one and steep final climbs, distanced twice in Vuelta observations
Uses breakaways for GC time accumulation rather than stage wins (Tour podium strategy)
Fades relative to stronger climbers in same breakaway groups on mountainous terrain
Protected as classification contender but lacks firepower to defend on hardest finales
form_trajectory: negative: consecutive Vuelta struggles on hard climbs suggest declining form or exposure at higher difficulty level compared to Tour podium performance
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 10
2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 7
